question_text: Write a program to swap the values of two variables.
title: Swap Two Integers
solutionCode: void swap(int* a, int* b) { int temp = *a; *a = *b; *b = temp; printf("a=%d, b=%d", *a, *b); }

question_text: Write a program which input three numbers and display the largest number using ternary operator.
title: Find Largest Ternary
solutionCode: void findLargest(int a, int b, int c) { int largest = (a > b) ? (a > c ? a : c) : (b > c ? b : c); printf("%d", largest); }

question_text: Write a program which accepts days as integer and display total number of years, months and days in it. for example : If user input as 856 days the output should be 2 years 4 months 6 days.
title: Convert Days to YMD
solutionCode: void convertDays(int totalDays) { int years = totalDays / 365; int remainingDays = totalDays % 365; int months = remainingDays / 30; int days = remainingDays % 30; printf("%d years %d months %d days", years, months, days); }

question_text: Any integer is input by the user. Write a program to find out whether it is an odd number or even number.
title: Check Odd Even
solutionCode: int isEven(int n) { if (n % 2 == 0) return 1; else return 0; }

question_text: Write a program to determine whether the seller has made profit or incurred loss. Also determine how much profit he made or loss he incurred. Cost price and selling price of an item is input by the user.
title: Profit or Loss Calculation
solutionCode: void calculateProfitLoss(float costPrice, float sellingPrice) { if (sellingPrice > costPrice) { printf("Profit of %.2f", sellingPrice - costPrice); } else if (costPrice > sellingPrice) { printf("Loss of %.2f", costPrice - sellingPrice); } else { printf("No Profit No Loss"); } }

question_text: Write a program to reveres any given integer number.
title: Reverse Integer Number
solutionCode: int reverseNumber(int n) { int reversed = 0; int isNegative = (n < 0); if (isNegative) n = -n; while (n > 0) { reversed = reversed * 10 + (n % 10); n /= 10; } return isNegative ? -reversed : reversed; }

question_text: Write a program to sum of digits of given integer number.
title: Sum of Digits

question_text: Write a program to check given number is prime or not.
title: Check Prime Number
solutionCode: int isPrime(int n) { if (n <= 1) return 0; for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) { if (n % i == 0) return 0; } return 1; }

question_text: Write a program to calculate HCF of Two given number.
title: Calculate HCF of Two Numbers
solutionCode: int calculateHCF(int a, int b) { while (b != 0) { int temp = b; b = a % b; a = temp; } return a; }

question_text: Write a program to enter the numbers till the user wants and at the end it should display the maximum and minimum number entered.
title: Min Max Tracker
solutionCode: void findMinMax(int arr[], int n) { if (n <= 0) return; int max = arr[0]; int min = arr[0]; for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (arr[i] > max) max = arr[i]; if (arr[i] < min) min = arr[i]; } printf("Maximum: %d, Minimum: %d", max, min); }

1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/4 + 1/5 +…………1/n

solutionCode: double calculateHarmonicSum(int n) { double sum = 0.0; for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { sum += 1.0 / i; } return sum; }

question_text:
Write a program to compute area of triangle. Sides are input by user.  
Area = sqrt(s\*(s-a)\*(s-b)\*(s-c))  
where s=(a+b+c)/2.
title: Triangle Area Calculator
solutionCode: double calculateTriangleArea(double a, double b, double c) { double s = (a + b + c) / 2.0; return sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c)); }

question_text: Write a program to implement the Number Guessing Game. In this game the computer chooses a random number between 1 and 100, and the player tries to guess the number in as few attempts as possible. Each time the player enters a guess, the computer tells him whether the guess is too high, too low, or right. Once the player guesses the number, the game is over.
title: Number Guessing Game
solutionCode: void guessNumber(int target, int guess) { if (guess < target) { printf("too low"); } else if (guess > target) { printf("too high"); } else { printf("right"); } }

question_text: Raising a number to a power p is the same as multiplying n by itself p times. Write a function called power that takes two arguments, a double value for n and an int value for p, and return the result as double value. Use default argument of 2 for p, so that if this argument is omitted the number will be squared. Write the main function that gets value from the user to test power function.
title: Calculate Power Function
solutionCode: double power(double n, int p) { double result = 1.0; for (int i = 0; i < p; i++) { result *= n; } return result; }

question_text:
Write a program that lets the user perform arithmetic operations on two numbers. Your program must be menu driven, allowing the user to select the operation (+, -, \*, or /) and input the numbers. Furthermore, your program must consist of following functions:

question_text: Write a program to find the sum and average of one dimensional integer array.
title: Array Sum and Average
solutionCode: void calculateSumAndAverage(int arr[], int n) { int sum = 0; for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { sum += arr[i]; } float average = (float)sum / n; printf("Sum: %d, Average: %.2f", sum, average); }

question_text: Write a program to reverse the element of an integer 1-D array.
title: Reverse Integer Array
solutionCode: void reverseArray(int arr[], int n) { for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { printf("%d%s", arr[i], (i == 0) ? "" : " "); } }

question_text: Write a program to find the largest and smallest element of an array.
title: Find Array Extremes
solutionCode: void findExtremes(int arr[], int n) { if (n <= 0) return; int min = arr[0]; int max = arr[0]; for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (arr[i] < min) min = arr[i]; if (arr[i] > max) max = arr[i]; } printf("Smallest: %d, Largest: %d", min, max); }

question_text:
Write a menu driven program with following option  
a. Accept elements of an array  
b. Display elements of an array  
c. Sort the array using insertion sort method  
d. Sort the array using selection sort method  
e. Sort the array using bubble sort method  
Write C functions for all options. The functions should have two parameters name of the array and number of elements in the array.
title: Array Sorting Menu
solutionCode: void sortArray(int arr[], int n, int choice) { int i, j, temp; if (choice == 1) { for (i = 1; i < n; i++) { temp = arr[i]; j = i - 1; while (j >= 0 && arr[j] > temp) { arr[j + 1] = arr[j]; j--; } arr[j + 1] = temp; } } else if (choice == 2) { for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) { int min_idx = i; for (j = i + 1; j < n; j++) if (arr[j] < arr[min_idx]) min_idx = j; temp = arr[min_idx]; arr[min_idx] = arr[i]; arr[i] = temp; } } else if (choice == 3) { for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) { for (j = 0; j < n - i - 1; j++) { if (arr[j] > arr[j + 1]) { temp = arr[j]; arr[j] = arr[j + 1]; arr[j + 1] = temp; } } } } for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { printf("%d ", arr[i]); } }

question_text: P is one-dimensional array of integers. Write a C++ function to efficiently search for a data VAL from P. If VAL is present in the array then the function should return value 1 and 0 otherwise.

question_text: Suppose a one-dimensional array AR containing integers is arranged in ascending order. Function should have three parameters : (i) array AR (ii) the number to be searched and (iii) the number of elements N in the array.
title: Binary Search Implementation

question_text: Suppose A, B, C are arrays of integers of size M, N, and M + N respectively. The numbers in array A appear in ascending order while the numbers in array B appear in descending order. Write a user defined function in to produce third array C by merging arrays A and B in ascending order. Use A, B and C as arguments in the function.
title: Merge Sorted Arrays

question_text: Suppose X. Y, Z are arrays of integers of size M, N, and M + N respectively. The numbers in array X and Y appear in descending order. Write a user-defined function in to produce third array Z by merging arrays X and Y in descending order.
title: Merge Descending Arrays
solutionCode: void mergeArrays(int X[], int M, int Y[], int N, int Z[]) { int i = 0, j = 0, k = 0; while (i < M && j < N) { if (X[i] > Y[j]) { Z[k++] = X[i++]; } else { Z[k++] = Y[j++]; } } while (i < M) { Z[k++] = X[i++]; } while (j < N) { Z[k++] = Y[j++]; } for (int idx = 0; idx < M + N; idx++) { printf("%d ", Z[idx]); } }

question_text:
Given two arrays of integers A and B of sizes M and N respectively. Write a function named MIX () with four arguments, which will produce a third array named C. such that the following sequence is followed.  
All even numbers of A from left to right are copied into C from left to right.  
All odd numbers of A from left to right are copied into C from right to left.  
All even numbers of B from left to right are copied into C from left to right.  
All old numbers of B from left to right are copied into C from right to left.  
A, B and C are passed as arguments to MIX (). e.g., A is {3, 2, 1, 7, 6, 3} and B is {9, 3, 5, 6, 2, 8, 10} the resultant array C is {2, 6, 6, 2, 8, 10, 5, 3, 9, 3, 7, 1, 3}
title: Array Mixing Algorithm
solutionCode: int* MIX(int* A, int M, int* B, int N) { int* C = (int*)malloc((M + N) * sizeof(int)); int head = 0, tail = M + N - 1; for (int i = 0; i < M; i++) { if (A[i] % 2 == 0) C[head++] = A[i]; } for (int i = 0; i < M; i++) { if (A[i] % 2 != 0) C[tail--] = A[i]; } for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { if (B[i] % 2 == 0) C[head++] = B[i]; } for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { if (B[i] % 2 != 0) C[tail--] = B[i]; } return C; }

question_text: Write a program to concatenate one string contents to another.
title: String Concatenation
solutionCode: void concatenateStrings(char dest[], char src[]) { int i = 0, j = 0; while (dest[i] != '\0') { i++; } while (src[j] != '\0') { dest[i++] = src[j++]; } dest[i] = '\0'; printf("%s", dest); }

question_text: Write a program to find a substring within a string. If found display its starting position.
title: Find Substring Position
solutionCode: int findSubstring(char* str, char* sub) { if (!*sub) return 0; char *p1 = str; while (*p1) { char *p1_begin = p1, *p2 = sub; while (*p1 && *p2 && *p1 == *p2) { p1++; p2++; } if (!*p2) return (int)(p1_begin - str); p1 = p1_begin + 1; } return -1; }

question_text: Write a program to multiply array A and B of order NxL and LxM
title: Matrix Multiplication
solutionCode: void multiplyMatrices(int N, int L, int M, int** A, int** B) { for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < M; j++) { int sum = 0; for (int k = 0; k < L; k++) { sum += A[i][k] * B[k][j]; } printf("%d ", sum); } printf("\n"); } }

question_text: Declare a structure to represent a complex number (a number having a real part and imaginary part). Write functions to add, subtract, multiply and divide two complex numbers.
title: Complex Number Operations
solutionCode: void calculateComplex(double r1, double i1, double r2, double i2, char op) { double resR, resI; if (op == 43) { resR = r1 + r2; resI = i1 + i2; } else if (op == 45) { resR = r1 - r2; resI = i1 - i2; } else if (op == 42) { resR = r1 * r2 - i1 * i2; resI = r1 * i2 + i1 * r2; } else { double denom = r2 * r2 + i2 * i2; resR = (r1 * r2 + i1 * i2) / denom; resI = (i1 * r2 - r1 * i2) / denom; } printf("%.2f + %.2fi", resR, resI); }

question_text:
Define a structure batsman with the following specifications:  
bcode 4 digits code number  
bname 20 characters  
innings, notout, runs integer type  
batavg it is calculated according to the formula -  
 batavg =runs/(innings-notout)  
calcavg() Function to compute batavg
title: Batsman Average Calculator
solutionCode: float calcavg(int innings, int notout, int runs) { if (innings == notout) return 0.0; return (float)runs / (innings - notout); }
